title: Advanced
outcome: Speak with authority, nuance, and professional control in leadership, strategy, presentation, and high-pressure settings.

chapters.title: Nuanced Grammar and Precision
chapters.summary: Refine advanced grammar choices to sound sharp, accurate, and intentionally professional.
lexicon: a small but important difference in meaning
to make a statement less direct or absolute
to talk about what may be true
the style of language for a situation
a phrase whose meaning is not literal
a statement that can be supported or challenged
lessons: use conditionals for policy, planning, and risk
show levels of certainty and doubt precisely
remove extra words without losing meaning
switch between warm, neutral, and executive tone
apply advanced grammar for accuracy under pressure

chapters.title: Leadership and Influence
chapters.summary: Lead meetings, handle conflict, persuade stakeholders, and communicate as a trusted professional.
lexicon: a person affected by a project or decision
to give responsibility to another person
an experienced person who guides another
a formal look at performance or progress
raising an issue to a higher level
lessons: open, guide, and close a meeting effectively
communicate choices with reasoning and ownership

chapters.title: Presentations, Writing, and Thought Leadership
chapters.summary: Present ideas, explain data, answer questions, and write clearly for professional impact.
lexicon: a spoken explanation given to an audience
a phrase that moves from one point to the next
a chart, slide, or image used to explain something
the order and shape of information
information used to support a point
to make others accept an idea
the storyline or flow of a message
the answer given after a question
lessons: respond to questions without losing control
Panels, Public Forums, and Thought Leadership
speak as an expert in public settings

chapters.title: Strategy, Culture, and Global Communication
chapters.summary: Communicate across teams, cultures, and strategic situations with maturity and adaptability.
lexicon: shared behavior and values in a group
movement from one state to another
professional relationships that help you grow
a clear long-term picture of the future
lessons: speak clearly when pressure is high
create credibility through words and actions
help a group generate and shape ideas
guide decisions even without senior power

chapters.title: Mastery and Independent Expression
chapters.summary: Think in English, explain abstract ideas, and perform under time pressure with control and voice.
contexts: speaking about abstract or high-level topics
lexicon: about ideas more than physical things
how you see yourself and present yourself
the image others connect with a person or company
smooth and effective use of language
stress caused by time or importance
a reasoned case for one side
a move to a higher role or level
lessons: reduce translation and speak more directly
show balance while defending a position
tell meaningful stories without losing structure
show advanced control across speaking tasks
